title: Anathallis minima
intro: Anathallis minima, also known as the small Anathallis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, and prevent infections. Bioactive constituents of Anathallis minima include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is commonly used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Anathallis minima is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Anathallis, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This genus is a part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, a group of orchids commonly known as the "pleurothallids." The family Orchidaceae is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants, with over 30,000 known species worldwide. The subtribe Pleurothallidinae includes around 1,000 species of orchids, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions.
